methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Chariot Defense is a Series A-stage frontier-tech company in the Defense & Security sector, founded by Adam Warmoth, having raised $41M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression, using the $41M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $82M. Peer-Median compares Chariot Defense against three same-sector, same-stage peers: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M, producing a median valuation of $300M with a range of $150M to $500M. This wider peer band reflects material dispersion in Defense & Security valuations at the Series A stage.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $70M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $191M. The Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively on capital deployed; the Peer-Median method reflects market pricing but carries elevated uncertainty given the heterogeneity in peer business models and technology maturity within Defense & Security. Key uncertainties include Chariot's revenue traction, proprietary IP differentiation, and customer concentration relative to the peer set.
